text_blocks.snippet: Reunification counselling in Vancouver and British Columbia (BC) is primarily provided through government-funded programs (MCFD) for families with child protection concerns, or specialized private practitioners for cases involving separation, divorce, and alienation.
Key Services and Providers in BC/Vancouver:

list.snippet: MCFD-Referred Services (No-Fee): These are intensive, time-limited, in-home/office services for families referred by the Ministry of Children and Family Development (MCFD).
Vancouver Family Preservation & Reunification Services (FSGV): Offers counselling, parenting education, and therapeutic supervised access to strengthen relationships and increase safety.
Touchstone Family Association: Provides in-home/office counselling for families in crisis, including parent-teen mediation.
Westcoast Family Centres Society: Offers Family Preservation and Reunification services, including support for Indigenous families and supervised visitation.
Hollyburn Family Services: Provides services to families with children under 19 with child protection concerns (Surrey/Vancouver).
Reconnect Families (Vancouver): Led by Bob Finlay, this group specializes in reunification counselling for families dealing with high-conflict divorce, child resistance to visitation, and alienation.
Bright Star Counselling (Port Moody): Offers supportive, play-based reunification sessions for children and parents, focusing on rebuilding bonds after separation/divorce.
Family Forward (Alyson Jones & Associates): Provides specialized therapy and coaching to reunify families.
Susan Gamache (REACH Program): Focuses on parent-child relationships disrupted by high conflict or alienation.
Leanne Toews: Provides a structured, phased approach to parent-child contact and reunification, often including virtual or in-person sessions.
S.U.C.C.E.S.S.: Offers individual and family counselling with services in Cantonese, Mandarin, Korean, and Farsi.
Family Justice Centres (FJC): Provide free services, including needs assessments, for families going through separation or divorce in BC. Bright Star Counselling +14

text_blocks.snippet: What to Expect:Reunification therapy often involves a team approach (case managers, counsellors for each parent, child therapy), in-home outreach, and parenting education to improve communication and rebuild trust. It is a process that focuses on the best interests of the child, addressing underlying issues like alienation, estrangement, or family breakdown. Reconnect Families +1

list.snippet: For MCFD-funded programs, a referral from a social worker is required.
Private services can be contacted directly for intake. Westcoast Family Centres +1
